reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. "Finite group G" is encoded as `[Group G] [Fintype G]`; the group ring RG is `MonoidAlgebra R G`; the element N = g₁ + ... + gₙ is `∑ g : G, MonoidAlgebra.of R G g`, since `MonoidAlgebra.of R G` sends each g to `single g 1`, the canonical basis embedding. Membership in `center (MonoidAlgebra R G)` resolves to `Subring.center`, which by `Subring.mem_center_iff` means commuting multiplicatively with every element — exactly the meaning of "in the center of the group ring." The universal quantification over arbitrary rings R and arbitrary finite groups G is captured by the type-variable signature. No hypotheses are dropped, added, or narrowed, and the conclusion matches directly.
